data.title: Freeport can resume copper concentrate exports with penalty, Indonesia minister says
data.description:
Indonesia's Energy and Mineral Resources Minister said Friday that the government has agreed to allow Freeport McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) to resume exports of copper concentrate until repairs at its smelter are completed, but would impose higher export duties on the shipments as a penalty.

The minister said the higher duties would be imposed because Freeport's (NYSE:FCX) smelter in Gresik had failed to operate on schedule after last October's fire, adding that the government was still calculating the export volume it would allocate to the company.

The government had given an exemption until the end of 2024 from its ban on exports of copper concentrate, as the company was ramping up production at its new Manyar smelter, but the smelter is still being repaired after the fire, and Freeport (FCX) cannot currently process copper concentrate at the plant; the company has requested an export quota of 1.3M tons this year to deal with its rising stocks of copper concentrate.

Freeport Indonesia (FCX) has ~400K tons of copper concentrate in its inventories, and estimated concentrate excess could potentially amount to 1.5M tons this year, CEO Tony Wenas said this week.

data.title: Beyond Oil expands 5-Year master distribution agreement with Latitude to Ukraine
data.description:
• Beyond Oil (OTCQB:BEOLF) has signed an agreement to expand its existing master distribution agreement with Latitude, adding Ukraine to the partnership's territory.
  • Under the expanded agreement, signed February 16, 2025, Latitude has committed to purchasing a minimum of $302,400 in 2025, with minimum annual purchase commitments increasing to reach $1.51M in 2029.
  • The agreement also includes an annual license fee of $100,000 starting from the second year of the agreement, which can be rebated if Latitude meets its annual purchase targets.
  • Over the five-year period covered by the agreement, Latitude has committed to purchase a minimum of $4.54M worth of Beyond Oil's product.
  • Under the terms of the agreement and its expansion, the agreement may be renewed for successive five-year terms on substantially the same terms and conditions.
  • Source: Press release
